title: SQL Injection (SQLi)
description: Exploit poorly sanitized database queries to extract sensitive data or bypass authentication.

lesson.summary: SQL Injection occurs when user input is incorrectly inserted into a database query. This can allow an attacker to read sensitive data, modify database entries, or execute administrative operations.
lesson.vulnerableCode:
const query = `SELECT * FROM users WHERE username = '${req.body.username}' AND password = '${req.body.password}'`;
db.execute(query);
lesson.secureCode:
const query = `SELECT * FROM users WHERE username = ? AND password = ?`;
db.execute(query, [req.body.username, req.body.password]);
mistakes: Concatenating strings to build SQL queries
Escaping characters manually instead of using parameterized queries
tips: Use least privilege for database accounts.
Regularly run SAST tools to detect SQLi.

arena.attackDescription: An attacker is attempting to extract the admin password hash by asking true/false questions to the database via the login form.
